channel.title: /biz/ Active (filtered, with replies)
channel.description: Filtered out: general, gme, bbbyq. OP + first 3 replies + last 30 replies.

item.title: Polkadot - First halving — 325 replies — 443.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
Polkadot - First halving

Open thread • Replies: 325 • Updated: 2026-04-14 14:55 UTC

OP

https://polkadothalving.com/ 10 more days. https://www.parity.io/blog/refining-polkadots-economic-architecture-issuance-DOT-DAP-and-network-adjustments
https://x.com/Polkadot/status/2028482336463945765
"
Polkadot’s economic upgrade begins rolling out in 10 days.

Enhanced tokenomics increases DOT scarcity and introduces new governance and staking mechanisms.

▸ DOT supply capped at 2.1B
▸ Emissions cut 53.6%
▸ Unbonding from 28 days to 24-48 hours
"

Early replies (first 3)

61933757
Guys?

61933779
>>61932834
10000$ @ 6.5
i dont sell my dotty

61936705
Guys?

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62107462
100$ end of bull run

62107808
>>62107462
75$
For me IMHO.

62109094
>>62107808
Very good my good sar Mumbai warrior sar. Yes yes. We shall eat with metal spoons once we reach the nirvana of 75$

Not sure if you guys noticed. https://staking.polkadot.cloud/#/overview - The APY is 5.33% and 53.14% of the supply is staked. Therefore the inflation = 5.33 * 0.5314 = 2.832362%. So the inflation is basically 2.83% now and I think the 5.33% is the AVERAGE of the last month therefore we still have like 1 or 3 more days until a full month will pass since the 1st halving. Not sure if the halving was on the 13th or the 15th. I know they said PI day and at the same time I'm not 100% if the actual drop happened on PI day... So yea. But what I'm saying is that the inflation dropped from like 8-10% to now 2.8%.

62109790
My good sars, the bottom is in. I hope you are staking for we are going to nirvana (75$) and beyond (100$). We feed the whole village of Mumbai. The thread has been alive for 40 whole days. Let's see if we can get it to 60 days. If we do then the Mumbai wairriors will eat for generations to come.

Best regards from Mumbai city (The capital of DOT),
Vishnu Kumar

62110141
>>62110117
>>62109790
Was this you my Mumbai brother?

62110170
>>62110117
not DOT, but yet another bridge exploit in the DOT eco

62110237
Jesuschrist, how can you all keep talking about this dead project? Just get over it. Even linkies have given up

62110621
>>62110117
What in the hell?

62110845
>>62110117
LOL
LMAO even

62111458
>>62110117
>>62110141
>>62110170
>>62110621
>>62110845
https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2026/04/13/attacker-mints-usd1-billion-polkadot-tokens-on-ethereum-ends-up-stealing-just-usd250-000
>Attacker mints $1 billion Polkadot tokens on Ethereum, ends up stealing just $250,000
>on Ethereum
>The Sunday exploit targeted the bridge contract, not Polkadot's core network. Polkadot's native token DOT was unaffected.
>The Sunday exploit targeted the bridge contract, not Polkadot's core network.
>not Polkadot's core network
>NOT Polkadot's core network
It was a issue on the side of ETH.

>>62110141
The Mumbai warriors strike yet again. WE SHALL EAT WITH METAL SPOONS FOR MILLENNIA

62111510
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
Another point is that if this happened on the side of DOT then governance could just revert the transaction. Hackers can only get away with this kind of stuff on ETH because ETH doesn't have a good governance. The funds of EVERY exploit on DOT were recovered. We can't say that for ETH or for any other ecosystem.

It's laughable how people try to portray DOT as getting hacked when in reality the exploit happened on ETH, not DOT.

Look at the picture this guy posted >>62110117. Why are the looking at the exploit on etherscan, if it's a DOT exploit? How come we see "Transfer 108 ETH from Uniswap V4: Pool..."? Do you notice what I mean? The exploit is on ETH, not DOT. And even if the exploit happened on DOT governance would freeze the funds and revert the transactions.

62111757
>>62111458
But how did he gets away minting that much? Who was asleep at the wheel?

62111917
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
Another thing. The exploit happened on Hyperbridge, on a smart contract, on the side of Ethereum. And Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT. Do you notice how we're basically 2 hops away from DOT?. Hyperbridge is a corechain on DOT (1st hop). Hyperbridge has smart contracts deployed on ETH in order for them to bridge between Hyperbridge and ETH (2nd hop). The exploit was an ETH smart contract exploit on Ethereum. If the same exploit happened on the side of Hyperbridge then the funds could EASILY be recovered through either the Hyperbridge governance (which is a copy of DOTs gov system. That's the whole point. That the corechains inherit DOTs functionality, therefore it's superior governance system) or the Polkadot governance.

>>62111757
Well.. Read the article https://www.coindesk.com/tech/2026/04/13/attacker-mints-usd1-billion-polkadot-tokens-on-ethereum-ends-up-stealing-just-usd250-000
>The accepted message executed changeAdmin on the bridged Polkadot token contract, transferring admin rights to the attacker's address. With admin control, the attacker minted 1 billion tokens in a single transaction and routed them through Odos Router V3 into a Uniswap V4 DOT-ETH pool, extracting roughly 108.2 ETH across what appears to be multiple swaps at slightly different prices.
He got the admin right to the contract, therefore he could do an infinite mint

62112179
>>62110117
>>62110170
>>62110237
>>62110621
>>62110845
https://x.com/hyperbridge/status/2043676775083803057
"
The attacker minted 1 billion bridged DOT tokens (over 2,800x the legitimate circulating supply (~356K tokens)), and sold them on a decentralized exchange.

We’re actively working with security partners to track and recover the affected funds.

Bridging will remain paused while additional safeguards are implemented and reviewed.
"
I guess they'll probably recover the funds.

62112771
>>62111917
>>62112179

Whelp DOT is finished. This is terrible publicity.

Normies won't bother reading in-depth. Just DOT HACKED

62112823
>>62112771
Well, there's no normies in the market... Oh well. Also, normies don't really have any money to begin with. Another thing is that having normies in your project isn't the best thing to begin with. Like, just look at your sentence
>Normies won't bother reading in-depth
>won't bother reading in-depth
>in-depth
So... in a sense you're implying that I want people who have no clue in DOT. That's the worst thing ever. Sure, they can buy in at 55$, but not at these prices. Normies never buy bottom, always the tops.

62112862
>>62112771
I'd guess what you're implying by "normies" are shallow people who don't know what they're doing. If people have no fucking clue what they're doing then why would I want those people around me? You're the average of your 5 closest friends. If the 5 closest people around me can't even write or read then I probably won't be able to do that as well. In a sense this is true. If you're smart you'll understand that every person has something that they're good and bad at. Just take the good and reject and reverse the bad. Again, the normies always come at high prices. There are almost NO normies at bear market bottoms. And this is most likely THE BOTTOM or at worst the bottom will be reached somewhere mid summer. And the bottom can't be far off. Worst case scenario I see is about 10-20% lower than the last local bottom of 60k$@BTC. So imagine a worst case scenario of 48k$ per BTC. But it'll just be a wyck that'll last for maybe a few minutes or like a couple of hours. I don't really see it going below 50k$ though. Probably like 52k$. And the normies won't start coming until like 100k$+

62112999
>>62112771
The thing is. Normies DO NOT decide anything. Think about it. Normies implies that they're in the middle of the herd. The 98% is ruled by the 2%. Why? Because the herd is asleep and unaware while the 2% is awake and gets stuff. People who understand stuff get to have the control.

62113306
>>61932834
Was it just hacked?

62113678
>>62113306
Maybe read the comments
>>62111458
>>62111510
>>62111917
>>62112179
DOT was NOT hacked. One of it's L2s got exploited and it wasn't even on the side of DOT. It was on the side of ETH. So to be real an ETH smart contract got exploited, NOT DOT.


Mumbai warriors... I believe our time has come. The thread doesn't refresh to page 1 anymore therefore I think this is it. When I write a message it doesn't refresh to the first page anymore. We had a good run my Mumbai warriors, my brethren. It will soon be time to say goodbye. We survived for a total of 41 day. We got 11.5k DOT + 11 * 150 = 13150 DOT. The whole of Mumbai will eat for MILLENNIA !!! With METAL spoons I tell you!

62113759
>>62112999
Nice trips

62113766
>>62113759
Tnx, I try Panjali. Panjali, it is time. The thread doesn't bump to the 1st page anymore. Today we dine like real man with METAL spoons. PolkaCurry for all the Mumbai warriors. We shall eat only the best in Mumbai.

62114066
My Mumbai warriors I've been able to pay Jeny Smith, the famous porn star, for a short video. My warriors, the women are flocking to us; https://thothub.mx/videos/1505023/jeny-smith-polka-dot-pantyhose/ - She is sponsored by POLKADOT. We're finally getting the advertisement we deserve and need in order to PENETRATE the market.

62114370
>>62111510
DOT should switch ocer to chainlink so stuff like a bridge hack won't happen. Chainlink is #1 bridge in the space. This is the sort of thing that can happen of you don't keep things tight with a professional grade bridge.

62114383
>>62114066
You were memeing this whole thread but it seems youre a genuine dot thread. Before this thread dies please tell us your genuine bull thesis for DOT my Mumbai brother. I saw DOT was included on the new commoditiy list. Does it involve RWA market capture?

62114389
>>62114066
Did you just start Mumbai larping to save face after the DOT inflation halving didn't instantly pump? If you are a real DOT head keep your chin up brotha, the bottom will be in october and next bullrun is 2029. If DOT can capture some of that RWA pie I'm sure it can go up from here, assuming it can somehow break itself out of this lull/show forth something impressive that seds it apart from other already established blocks like SOL, ETH or AAVE.

62114813
Is this the record for a thread
A Monero general got 40 days once I think.

62115213
>>62114813
PolkaDOT is the future of finance for a reason

item.title: Make it House or Apartment — 39 replies — 156.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
Make it House or Apartment

Open thread • Replies: 39 • Updated: 2026-04-14 15:33 UTC

OP

What are your "modest" make it house requirements? Let's say you made $10,000,000 and are maybe willing to spend $2,000,000 on it, what are your non-negotiables? Which of mine seem stupid?

P.S. Try to be realistic and balance cost/maintenace with need - do you REALLY need an tennis court and a pool if there is a pleb-center with both within a mile?

> a house or condo in a good area that rents for at least $2,000+ (currently) without much hood risk. This isn't profitable (doesn't pay for itself in 10 years), but it's basically what pays for your house in perpetuity. You can do this with non-RE assets, but this seems to be the only way that's resistant to all bullshit like inflation, economic downturns, etc (P.S. that's why it might also be beterr off as a 4-unit - can't be too luxury or economy eats it. Condos and townhoses are much less of a pain in the ass, but the association might make a rule against renting. So maybe a small house in a nice area, but then you have to contract all the grass bullshit.

> high ceilings so your farts dissipate, enough said

> good air and water circulation / filtering. Would be cool to have full NBC, but that's probably a million on its own. So just a good air filter, maybe some detectors of critical issues, a really good water filter, good water pressure so if one person turns on hot water the other doesn't lose it (we poors know)

> serviceable cable channels and overall well-done, modular electric so it can be replaced. Would kind of be nice to have wired internet accross the house. Delusion tier is faraday cages on some rooms

Early replies (first 3)

62111279
> 2 car garage that ACTUALLY fits two cars and maybe a side area for tools. Maybe two separate doors to keep maintenance cheap. 3-car seems to be pushing it. I would maybe have a lift or a service tunnel, but again, not sure I would want to work on cars at that point. Very divided on good heating and ventilation here. Very expensive if not used to service cars.

> two-three bathrooms, guest room that can sit unused, muh office, her office, master bedroom, maybe one more bedroom, kitchen, dining room, work out room (basement or one with one glass / slider side?) this all fits in 2000ft^2

> somehow green area but within 15 minutes of city. On a hill, preferrably

> at least an acre with wooded, private back

> brick or stone, no fucking vinyl or whatever the fuck

> pic very unrelated, don't really want a smelly shared lake or river, which is all you get in 2 mil range

Dat pink id though. Looks like all I am getting is a dragon-dildo, lube, and strobelights trap room.

62111331
If I was an American who'd just made it, I'd buy a plot of land and build a house out of REINFORCED CONCRETE instead of plywood.

62111366
>>62111273

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62113985
>>62111273
500k USD. I just want a cozy house in a big land plot. And i want to leave enough money to hire guards, house staff, and a big titty fem cook so i can drink milkshakes straight from her fat tits.

62114085
>>62111273
Two words. Inside mountain.

62114100
I have a finished basement with a laser projector and comfy couch, and I have a son. I've made it.

62114107
>>62111273
A 2M isnt that nice. Its just the minimum to be respectable.

62114110
>>62114100
i am failing at life but i am financially stable so nobody notices or cares. the only thing that matters in western society is money. if you had a loving family and a kind soul and you were poor, you would be called a failure.

62114116
>>62111273
needs to be at least 1/4 sq miles of land, preferably more
plenty of trees, high quality well, ideally a little lake or pond with fish (for feeding and being frens, not killing)
must get at least 50" of rainfall per year, preferably over 100"
temps between 30*F and 80*F 99% of the time
must be close to the ocean
as few neighbors as possible

yep SE Alaska would be perfect if not for all the richfags who already built their shitty summer homes all over the islands there

62114117
>>62114100
You’re a loser.

62114269
>>62111273
>Apartment, Condo
Anons are so demoralized they fantasize about sharing walls

62114279
>>62114116
Could try coastal Washington or Oregon.

62114294
>>62111273
100% white within 5 miles.
And no Mexican gardeners showing up in my area either.
100% white.
(I already have this in Europe)

62114298
>>62111574
>I also question a hot tub
I use mine a lot. 20+ times a year. We clean it every week or two.
Americans use chemicals to clean their hottub only once every 6 months.
Disgusting.
Just water in mine.
We heat it with wood haha. Another thing Americans can't do.

62114299
>>62114294
Europeans are cucks.

62114311
I own my modest make it home and bought it cash.
Solid two storied brick house from 1900, new roof, new exterior wood work, 180m2, big garden with both a grill patio, vegetable plot, rose beds and garden pavilion.
In the county side with horses and wheat Fields as "nNEIGhbours".
It's not a mansion, it's basically boomer core, but that works for me.A pool and sauna would complete it.

62114312
>>62111531
High ceilings keep cooling costs low.

62114317
>>62114117
And you're brown. GG NO RE

62114356
>>62114299
>he said, choking back his tears

62114357
I just want a cabin by a stream. Could sit here for hours just listening to the running water.

62114369
>>62111331
>1pbtid forced meme thread derailer
>>62111366
based

62114373
>>62111582
the future is cold and uncertain o_o

62114375
>>62114110
>if you had a loving family and a kind soul and you were poor, you would be called a failure.
i almost guarantee you wouldn't FEEL like one, though
there used to be poor communities/towns in north & south america which were very nice to live in anyway
but again, world is becoming colder

62114378
>>62111273
This would be my make it house
I'd retire in Japan.

62114384
>>62114357
very based
i always loved little creeks/streams, there was one in the pine woods i used to sit by as a kid
sometimes i'd jump across the rocks

62114392
>>62114356
>He pretends there's not an identical cafeteria at Ikea

62114413
For me it's 1960s ranch house with 2car garage, and bbq pool party with neighbors, and real trick or treating

;_;

62114437
>>62111331
jesus yuropoors are still seething, what are they mad about now? did the war disrupt their semen rations?

62114438
>>62114413
damn..

and to answer the OP >>62111273
i always liked the idea of a penthouse apartment, but i've never even lived in a city lol

i also like the idea of a villa, the kind with a small enclosed courtyard in the center
i'd like a lot of space rooms for projects and other stuff, i picture a ground floor (or basement) with a garage & several other no-frills rooms without many windows, with all the rooms to actually live in on the above 2-3 floors
but i don't know how that layout would work with a courtyard...

62114439
>>62114438
>a lot of space rooms for projects
a lot of space for projects*

62115260
>>62111273
I made $30MM and bought a house for under $2MM, cheap city, low cost

>5100 square feet
>Giant ass paver driveway. It's got a loop in the front but you can take a straight shot to the garage, like a lowercase h. This is great because if you have friends, nannies, housekeepers whatever over they can park in the loop without blocking your ability to get in and out.
>Giant ass kitchen. Subzero fridge, freezer hidden behind custom Amish walnut cabinetry that extends throughout, taj mahal quartz countertops, wolf GAS oven WITH real ass mega ventilation so you can vent like a champ when you sear steak. There's a pot filler above the range but that's kinda a meme.
>Kitchen pantry with a second sink, giant countertops, under sink ice maker, outlets, storage etc. Huge because you now have space for your basic kitchen appliances without gumming up your kitchen. You go to a poor people kitchen and it's fucking packed with loads of shitty countertop appliances and you don't have space to prep or do shit.
>Second fridge/freezer adjacent to the pantry so if you're salting a rib roast you've got space for that.
>Upstairs has a wing for homeschooling. So you've got a room with tons of storage and two study carrels, with a built in cabinet for a printer to slide in and out. Then a playroom for the kids and then a final room for a classroom.
>Walk-in closets in every bedroom
>Heated bathroom floors
>Comfy gas fireplaces
>Multi zone cooling so you can cool bedrooms nicely at night but not bother with the rest of the house
>Good sound proofing throughout
>Wired for access points with everything connecting in a walk in utility closet large enough for a server rack
Etc

62115298
>>62111273
>>62111366
>>62111548
>>62114357
>>62114384
>>62114413
>>62114438
Brown people and niggers are near all of these places. In fact you can't even maintain your own yard without brown laborers you lazy fucking kikes.

62115315
>>62114100
as long as you’re stable theres not much more to ask for out of life
>>62114356
what a beautiful place. we truly live in a land of plenty.

item.title: A Once In A Generation Celebration edish >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices >Educational sites: >Fin… — 22 replies — 88.0/hr
item.content:encoded:
A Once In A Generation Celebration edish >Some helpful links to numb the pain >European indices >Educational sites: >Fin…

Open thread • Replies: 22 • Updated: 2026-04-14 15:38 UTC

OP

A Once In A Generation Celebration edish

>Some helpful links to numb the pain
https://findahelpline.com/
https://www.nvidia.com/refunds/
https://www.whitehouse.gov/refunds
https://jlingz.com/

>European indices
https://live.euronext.com/en
https://www.justetf.com/en/how-to/invest-in-europe.html

>Educational sites:
https://www.investopedia.com
https://www.khanacademy.org/economics-finance-domain

>Financial TV Streams:
https://www.newslive.com/american/cnbc.html
https://www.livestreamy.net/bloomberg/

>Charts:
https://www.tradingview.com
https://www.finscreener.com
https://www.portfoliovisualizer.com

>Screeners: so you feel like in the movies
https://finviz.com/
https://etfdb.com/

>Gambling:
https://www.optionsplaybook.com/options-introduction/
https://www.optionsprofitcalculator.com
https://optionstrat.com/
https://www.optionistics.com/quotes/option-prices

>Pre-Market and Live data:
https://www.investing.com/indices/indices-futures
https://finance.yahoo.com/

>Calendars
https://www.marketwatch.com/economy-politics/calendar
https://www.earningswhispers.com/calendar
https://www.cmegroup.com/trading/interest-rates/countdown-to-fomc.html
https://www.mortgagenewsdaily.com/data/building-permits

>Boomer Investing 101: so zoomers with meme stocks can outperform you
https://www.bogleheads.org/wiki/Getting_started
https://www.sec.gov/search-filings

>Misc smg:
https://www.financialjuice.com/home
https://finance.yahoo.com/trending-tickers
https://market24hclock.com/
https://www.dividendchannel.com/drip-returns-calculator
https://brokerchooser.com/
https://www.chathamfinancial.com/technology/us-market-rates

Previously on /smg/:>>62114582

Early replies (first 3)

62115296
Waiting for captchas is bearish

62115299
Nixon did know his stuff about Iran doe. We need to install a friendly shah

62115300
TOLD YOU RETARDS AGAIN UP AND TO THE RIGHT. HOPE YOU LISTENED

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62115302
Uh oh Oil Baggies

KEK KEK KEK

If you liked oil at $120, surely you'll love it at $60. KEK OIL BAGGOTS

:)

62115305
Lots of dumb mumus in here celebrating over a couple soundbites from blatantly biased media. If you like money, now's the time to go long oil and short equities, especially US tech. Buy the dips in Asian stocks when shortages start and people start panic selling.

62115306
A Toast!

To Mr. Bobos arrival!

To the greatest and biggest party anyone has ever seen!

62115307
The fuck is going on as of lately?! That last thread had way too much talk about ladyboys going on. I miss when sister mating was the thing. This ladyboy stuff is too confusing, it makes me feel strange...

62115308
>>62115302
TRVST THE PLQN...The squeeze is coming we will win against the treasury...they are manipulating the price...

62115309
>>62115299
YOU NEED TO INSTALL 0DTE SPY AND QQQ CALLS. THERES STILL TIME

62115310
>>62115299
shah fren or or sheikh fren

62115311
>>62115299
YOU NEED TO INSTALL 0DTE SPY AND QQQ CALLS. THERES STILL TIME
>>62115305
LMAO ANOTHER RETARD REKT BOBO TELLING PEOPLE TO SHORT INTO AN UPMOVE. ABSOLUTE BRAINDEAD RETARD MONKEY KEK BAGGIE

62115312
>>62115299
Iran doesn't want a monarchy

62115316
folio is at ATH again
I know I could have profited more from this war but I'm up 14% YTD it feels really good

62115317
Dearest Bobo.
It is with a heavy heart that I must inform you of salt and pepper's imminent breach of the 7000 threshold.
It seems your vigorous and ladylike protests of market manipulation and arm waving that things should not be thus did little to delay the rushing brazen bulls.
Yours truly, Mumu.

62115319
alright sandisk, its not funny anymore, START PUMPING AAAAAAAAHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHHH

62115323
>>62115317
BOBO IS TOO RETARDED TO UNDERSTAND THIS

62115324
> nows

62115325
>>62115319
Sorry bro $1000 is too big of a mind barrier gotta wait for their 1:10 stock split thanks for playing

62115326
>>62115305
Nobody is panic selling because of some sand people. I also blame the mass use of ETF.

62115331
NAME ONE REASON TO PANIC SELL STOCKS WHEN THEY WILL ONLY GO HIGHER QUICKLY DUE TO INSANE INFLATION AND DOLLAR DEVALUATION. THERES NOWHERE TO GO. BUY 0DTE CALLS ON THE INDEX NOW BEFORE YOU GET LEFT BEHIND. IF YOU DONT BUY 0DTES EVERY DAY YOU CANT EVEN BEAT INFLATION

62115332
>signed up for IPo alerts from fidelity
>every single one has been utter shit

need to get in on the ground floor of the next BIG THING

62115333
>>62114726
>IF U WANNA FIND A WIFE PICK A FAT BITCH WITH LOW SELF ESTEEM AND U WILL LIVE A HAPPY LIFE
Terrible financial advice. I did this and she turned into a gigantic bitch and a gold digger demanding to be a trophy wife. They all have Tiktok now and deserve an easy life on your dime.
"Dating" is a humiliation ritual and you will lose all your gains. Hoeflation is out of control.

item.title: A sincerely serious question for experienced /biz/nessman — 76 replies — 71.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
A sincerely serious question for experienced /biz/nessman

Open thread • Replies: 76 • Updated: 2026-04-14 14:35 UTC

OP

Can someone explain this shit to me? I know that stock market != real economy, and I know that market can remain irrational longer than I can remain solvent yadadada. But isn't it in theory, supposed to at least loosely map to fundamentals and signals?
There is no end to war.
The ceasefire is tentative and unlikely to hold.
Even if we miraculously reach to a conclusion soon:
The disruptions and shortages will last for many months.
A significant chunk of world's energy infrastructure is destroyed and will take years to rebuild.
Shortage of fuel and its inflationary effects will continue for the rest of the year at minimum.
Fertilizer prices will inevitably cause food inflation during the next harvest.
Further shortages and disruptions of aluminum and helium will affect many industries.
And yet the market is incredibly calm and constantly rallying behind even the most obvious "damage control for the markets" news. Or hell even to the most negative news. The DOW is almost back to where it was late February. Why is this the case? The only explanations I can think of are:
We are in the middle of what would otherwise have been a legendary bull run and even the colossal fuck ups in the world stage can't fully blunt it. It's simply absorbing every negative signal and keep pushing forwards with incredible momentum, only slowed down a bit.
The economy, at this point is under total control of a handful of massive institutional investors like Blackrock and other funds. This historically unprecedented consolidation turned it into a fake gay circus. They call the shots, every other player is liquidity fodder for them. And they are unwilling to call the bottom yet, so the show goes on.
There is no conspiracy. Market is remaining irrational for a prolonged period for some reason, but eventually it will react appropriately to current shortages and price accordingly.
What else could be? What do you think? Help this retard understand.

Early replies (first 3)

62112281
AI is fucking here. It is that simple

62112285
>>62112276
who cares nerd, line goes up

62112299
The market runs on fake news headlines now. Perception is reality nigga and reality is looking pretty good! Iran's military no longer exists and now they have no economy. That's what matters. Gas prices, unemployment, and the other real world issues you are worried about only affect the goyim.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62112973
All that bullshit you posted is already riced in faggot

62113200
>>62112956
No, the inflation expectation is. Right now if you're not a complete fucking retard you should know that sometime this year the economy will start hurting real bad and that the government will attempt to solve it with the money printer and that'll pump the markets. COVID is exactly the situation you should be looking at. yeah there was a big crash, but was immediately followed by an insane gigapump. Even if you bought at the absolute peak right before the crash, you'd be in the money before the end of the year and most likely have made a lot of gains. Knowing that, the rational strategy is to just buy every single dip and that prevents the markets from going into exactly the kind of crash that everyone assumes a rational market would go in right now.

62113451
>>62113200
the inflation expectation did not dramatically change in the last two weeks that would cause a 12% pump. So, no, this isn't explaining the recent price action and sentiment for me.

62113460
>>62113231
it's over bobo, maybe learn to read charts? this was a textbook technical rally

62113485
>>62113451
>for me.

62113587
>>62113485
Uhh yeah exactly? What's your point? Everything here is subjective and up to personal interpretation.

62113626
>>62113587
No anon, the blockade of Hormuz is in fact not subjective. Its an actual event that is currently taking place and the longer it goes on the more damage can be expected to be done. You thinking otherwise is just you being retarded and not understanding how the world works.

62113876
>>62112448
Pure fucking speculation. The market is random and those who haven’t figured it out yet stay poor.

62113909
>>62112276
>under total control of a handful of massive institutional investors
You answered your own question

Also:
>>62112281
There’s AI programs that do a lot of the day trading at these big conglomerates. They decide when to buy or sell based on data they get from other big conglomerates that are… using AI for day trading.

Carrot -> stick -> forever

62114045
>>62113626
Yeah no shit. What I’m referring to is the narrative that anon provided. The guy I was responding is saying ‘inflation’ is the reason it bounced back from $630 end of March. That interpretation is subjective and not one I find satisfactory. Frankly, it’s fucking retarded.

At least read the conversation chain before replying. The rationale driving price action and prediction of future action is a subjective thing up to personal interpretation.

62114062
>>62113626
I guess you're the same retard that suggested 'inflation' caused the sudden pivot and bounce back near ATH on March 30th.

I can't believe you're seriously suggesting that everyone just woke up and suddenly realized inflation is gonna happen with higher oil prices, despite it downtrending into the war all month prior, and just rushed to bid it back up. Surely you can see why that's an idiotic rationale for the movement?

62114067
>>62112276
Anon this is what happens when you can print and direct funds into the stock market. The only thing that would stop this is if the dollar grift comes to an end.

62114087
>>62112276
>There is no end to war.
>The ceasefire is tentative and unlikely to hold.
clown world struck again

62114089
>>62114045
>>62114062
>this is subjective but also you're wrong

I don't think you actually understand what subjective means. It doesn't mean that there's room for interpretation because we don't have the full picture of everyone's trading strategy. There are objectively things happening in the real world. Even if completely random that would be objectively what is happening. There actually are correct narratives about what is driving the price movements.

62114090
CYCLES, ignore whatever bullshit, I'm not reading all that text.
This was a scandal cycle, because of the scandal the market heard there's a scandal and now it must dump. There were recovery attempts and most of them failed, and there were also crashing attempts and they all failed, but at the end of the scandal cycle REGARDLESS of what is actually happening anywhere there's a recovery cycle and a cooldown until the next scandal cycle.

This is just how it is, the market is cyclic automatic trading and at some point one cycle switches to the next.
>But this real world event!
In the real world I would kill you.

62114096
>>62114089
>There actually are correct narratives about what is driving the price movements.
Any narrative is a subjective interpretation of reality since we don't have complete information. Your narrative is inflation, that's fine but it's retarded. There is the objective reality of the world and market state, but beyond that it's subjective interpretation and rationalizations.

62114114
>>62114096
Yeah you fundamentally misunderstand what subjectivity is and seem to have confused with speculation.

62114115
>>62114089
Also, not saying you're wrong I'm saying it's retarded. That's my subjective interpretation. JESUS fucking Christ man learn to read and understand the world around you. The only 'correct' narrative is that there were more buyers than sellers. We don't have enough information to infer further, but a sudden shift in the sentiment concerning inflation is unlikely, imo.

62114122
>>62114114
No, I do not - look up the definition of the word. At any rate, if it helps you understand the point being made, replace it with whatever word gets the same point across for you, since you already know what I'm saying and instead of responding to it you've reduced your stance to a pathetic and failed attempt at pedantry.

62114131
>>62114122

speculate

1
a: to meditate on or ponder a subject : reflect
b: to review something idly or casually and often inconclusively
2: to assume a business risk in hope of gain
especially : to make a relatively risky investment in something (such as stocks or real estate) in the hope of making a large short-term profit from market fluctuations

transitive verb

>>>>>1: to take to be true on the basis of insufficient evidence : theorize
2: to be curious or doubtful about : wonder
speculates whether it will rain all vacation

62114135
>>62114131
Yes, those definitions of subjective fit exactly how I've been using the word. The soundness of a narrative is subjective, the formulation of it is a speculative process. I'm honestly not sure where you're confused here.

62114168
>>62112276
It’s simple.
There were enough retards shorting that someone decided to liquidate them all by pumping it.

62114194
>>62114168
surely that will be the end of it.

62114235
>trump acquires the straight of hormouz
>stock market

62114239
forgot pic

62114476
>>62112276

Big players are trading with bots, mwe have more stupid day traders that trade with scripts. They set the price according to their astrology patterns shits. Stocks reach the price like a shitty ebay bid.
big bots panic and bring down the price, stupid day traders panic and bring down the price, good investors buy the dip, bots unpanic and bring the price up back and +10%. Daytraders cry in the casino.
me making a lot of money. Notice how billionaires use their media corps to manipulate prices and create panic shit. Just play this game like a sociopath jew/politician and you will be fine. Remember when the Ai bubble shit was everywhere and suddenly those fuckers got their positions and now is going back... my only problem is I dont have enough money to buy every time those fuckers crash something.

62115121
>>62112575
Yeah trump sweets dont do shit anymore, it was fun while it lasted. Only way he can affect shit now is threatening a nuke or something and even that probably wont work again. He is crying wolf as long as its profitable and taking bribes left and right. No one bas sold out the country more than the Trump admin. What a fucking rug pull lmao

62115145
>>62114476
>bots arent scripts
My bot made over100% last year thank you very much.
Its akually “easy” to write profitable programs when your pie is at or UNDER a million bucks.
Good programs are hard to write past that point cause you actually make waves in the marketplace, accidentally break laws, liquidity for the trades you want to make, and there are only X many stocks/equities you can reasonably bot. 250k is the sweet spot. The further you are away from that the harder it is to be be successful

62115161
>>62112276
Simple. The market is not pricing reality, it's pricing another reality prompted by truthposts in ALL CAPS.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62115015

so now it should go down

62115026
I will be liquidated in the next candle, most likely. All my portfolio, gone. My crypto journey ends today. It was fun but it is what it is

62115044
>>62115026
just close it and reopen it with the same leverage at this same price, you'll get a higher liquidation number and can make the losses back by closing and reopening it as it falls back down.

62115063
>>62115044
I have paralysis. The losses are huge and after so many years in crypto doing relatively fine, this war has break my mind. It's unreal. I can't move.

62115076
>the entire weekly measured move is complete on a tuesday morning

this is blatant bait to tempt retail into buying ath

62115089
>>62115063
your frontal lobe is malfunctioning. you just need to press that button, it's better than losing all your money. then make it back by closing and reopening while it drops. This is a better strategy than losing all your money.

62115096
haha

62115137
Unironically why is it going up?

62115143
Patience padawans my time will come again

62115165
>>62115026

62115304
>>62115143
see you in 3 years, niggerbobo

62115313
>>62114780

item.title: What’s the best way to get simp money with AI girls? — 9 replies — 6.9/hr
item.content:encoded:
What’s the best way to get simp money with AI girls?

Open thread • Replies: 9 • Updated: 2026-04-14 14:21 UTC

OP

What’s the best way to get simp money with AI girls? What’s the best current software?

I’d like to get in on this before it’s cracked down on and or too late to do so. Is it already too late? I know OF makes you verify your identity now with IDs and facial recognition and such.

Early replies (first 3)

62114390
>>62114192
i make about $1.4k per month generating AI waifu content and posting it on fanbox/patreon, doesn't sound like much but in europe it covers rent and still leaves me with money for groceries and other stuff, it's super easy cause i automated the whole thing with python scripts so barely have to touch it except for occasional errors, just push your content until you reach at least 50 paying users for the top plan ($15-20), after that it’s basically passive

62114408
>>62114390

Thanks. How do you shill your content? Just posting on instagram and tiktok, or do you do ads somehow?

Also if you have many different accounts can you double or triple your revenue?

62114423
>>62114192
Who are these stupid niggers that pay for this stuff. In eastern europoor you could hold a gun to my head and i aint paying for nothing digital.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62114459
>>62114423
god knows

62114462
>>62114423
Rich Indians

I’m not kidding

62114475

62114480
>>62114390

like art/porn which looks hand-drawn? or something else?

OP

62114988
>>62114930

ill keep buying as pay cheques come in as long as we stay around the 4 year SMA

ill stop buying when we start running up and instead start selling to buy other assets, probably pay off my mortgage

62115186
>>62114930
i cope with my 5 stack and tell myself in 10 years i'll make it.

62115188
>>62114930
350 BTC or so.

Feels good. I'm living pretty modestly in a $2MM 5000 square foot house in a low cost state. Probably going to build a home gym extension with a sauna and hyperbaric oxygen chamber. Also a small fallout shelter just to be comfy.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62115225
>>62114930
Of course. My bitcoins are safe and sound in my hardware wallet.

item.title: Why aren’t you holding BTC? — 97 replies — 39.8/hr
item.content:encoded:
Why aren’t you holding BTC?

Open thread • Replies: 97 • Updated: 2026-04-14 13:13 UTC

OP

If you’re still watching or waiting to buy BTC, why not do it now?
>I guess you’re wasting time, or maybe you just prefer altcoins. What’s your reason?

Early replies (first 3)

62087977
>>62087937
I only watch it to laugh at Saylor and you bitsteinbaggies. Not buying digital nothing.

62087993
>>62087937
Waiting for sub60K

62087997
>>62087937
I don't have enough money for it to make me rich. Alts have a good chance of vastly outperforming BTC during the next big pump. I've decided to take the potential for a 10x over the guarantee of a 2x.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62102324
>>62102223
Yeah and being able to interact with DeFi there with millions of users while keeping self custody makes everything feel a lot safer and more seamless.

62102341
>>62091734
You should have ordered healthy foodies kek

62102346
>>62102233
a teenager

62102364
>>62102249
V3 is solid. V4 just went live a few weeks ago. Deposits are still ramping up. Kinda pointless comparing that to years of development. Give it time anon.

62102377
>>62095458
reread it anon

62102716
>>62087997
I made this same mistake in the last cycle. Trust me, all the liquidity will go into BTC, so it's better to buy and keep it on TBVs for yield instead of going for alts

62103228
>>62097432
The smuggest are newfags because they're trying to build their own conviction by mimicking the toxic maximalsm during the fork wars. The oldfags have either all left or have the ability to laugh at one's own expense. Bitcoin still hasn't done anything meaningful and "orange pilling" your retarded friends doesn't help. I consider myself a closet maxi because I do believe in bitcoin but I'd never associate with them or go to a meetup/conference.

62103805
>>62095600
For many bullish reasons

62103981
>>62095620
And are you expecting what then?

62105081
>>62100014
Depends on your goal.

62105096
I DCA into Bitcoin. It can either go up or it can either go down. I'd rather take my chances than holding gold for 30 years for a +1k profit

62106216
>>62095867

62106279
>>62106216

62106298
btc is going to 20k if not lower

>>62097398
>'good alts' will vastly outperform

there was like 3 alts that outperformed btc out of literally millions. your odds are terrible for alts, much better chance to just throw it all on red and call it a day

62106312
>>62106298

62106435

62106463
>>62097125

62107082
>>62106463

You're not smart enough.

62108172
>>62095759

62109768
>>62097398

You’ll wish you just held the hardest asset instead of playing ETF cosplay.

62110223
Guarantee 30% return on semiconductors and useless AI tech or gamble that Bitcoin crawls back to 100k any time soon despite losing 40% of it's value in the last few months. Hmmmm

62110242
I expected that until December Bitcoin will sunk to below 40k and Solana will be around $20. I will buy solana and Hold it until 2029 when the next bullrun will start.

2027-2028 will be crab market, everyone will say that crypto is dead just like 2023.

I will buy SOL because it will probably 20x while BTC will only 5x.

62110338
>>62102233

62110351
>>62110338

62110380
>>62087937

item.title: HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this? — 72 replies — 38.2/hr
item.content:encoded:
HoW iS eVerYonE dOinG this?

Open thread • Replies: 72 • Updated: 2026-04-14 13:46 UTC

OP

I love watching normie carhag/carfag compilations where Normies complain about how everything is too expensive.

I live on ~24k a year in a HCOL city making $26/hr
>$820 rent utilities included
>free transportation (fare fags aren’t checking train during my commute hours)
>$300/mo groceries (steak and potatoes mostly)
>$60/mo unlimited data
>$15/mo rent insurance
>$30 dental insurance *shrugs*

I regularly spend $8 on coffee, $15 on cigs, $30 dollars on pizza, and I STILL usually dump minimum $1500 a month into my brokerage account. Next month my credit limit will go from 12k>24k when I open another chase CC. I have 6 months expenses in a savings account and the equivalent in dry powder in my brokerage.

Being completely unstressed/concerned about money and whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card on cutie coffee sluts 4 times a week to order my $9 dollar 24oz drum of double chocolate mocha and then ripping a Newport 100 on a comfy bench while i enjoy my coffee is all the pleasure I need in this world

Early replies (first 3)

62107190
It's funny because I'm supposed to be an Europoor but my life standars is better than 99% of Americans lmfao.

I've never heard a single European complaining about debt. But you meet one of those Amerimutts that come to our continent for tourism or to teach English, and 5 minutes into the conversation they will start telling how how much in debt they are. BAFFLING.

62107202
>>62107169
>HCOL city
>$820 rent utilities included
and then your dad stopped molesting you and the out of body disassociation cope ended.

62107317
>>62107169
>>62107190
I'm American. Envious, ungrateful, whiny, bitchy Americans are an embarrassment. It's really not hard to live here at all. The problem is almost every retard that lives here thinks you should be able to afford a 2,300 sqft house on 1 acre of land, while having a new Toyota Camry in the driveway and enough in savings to go on 2 - 3 week-long vacations a year after putting 15% into an investment account, all on a McDonald's wage, or a basic-bitch corporate cubicle job, while only working 30 hours a week (yes, even 40 hours is too much work for Americans now).

Most people live WAY above their means. It's just a mixture of low-IQ combined with a set of personality traits that make them susceptible to short-term thinking and hedonism. Then they beg for free-gibs from the government while producing less material goods and services. It doesn't take a rocket scientist to figure out where this leads to.

I feel zero pity or sympathy for any of these people. Fuck them and their retarded choices.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62108106
>>62108081
Depends on the type. Ribeye is around 30 dollars, Sirloin is around 17 dollars, and sizzle steak is around 12 dollars.

62108113
>>62108103
I thought it was a credit card debt thread
>whipping out my girthy 12k CL sapphire preferred card

62108121
>>62107169
One way I save some money eating out with people while visiting a city is putting the bill on my card and having the others pay for the tax and tip in their splits.

62108129
>>62107387
>>larping zoomers who are either living at home or living in a total shit hole that’s worse than Soviet-Era commieblocks.
Yet they have enough money to pay for an expensive smartphone and data plan.

62108201
>>62108129
Debt, specifically credit card debt. Everybody (all age groups) want to look wealthy instead of having money to "keep up".

62108666
>>62108025
>Don’t have friends or drink the occasional beer
>Live like an incel hermit in your 20s so you can be slightly more successful in your 40s (maybe)
>Do not develop your social skills even though all high-paying jobs require charisma and nepotism
>Escape the matrix
Yeah man, you got it all figured out. Don’t Google why fraternity bros earn more money over the course of their lifetime than the average college graduate.

62108694
>>62108129
>Yet they have enough money to pay for an expensive smartphone and data plan.
It’s not 2012 anymore boomer. Unlimited talk/text/data is the basic plan, and you save maybe 5 dollars going for anything less. Also, most people are running around with 3 year old iPhones and only upgrade when they get the (((free upgrade))) that locks them into the plan they were gonna continue paying for anyways.

The iPhone hasn’t gotten better in 8 years. Nobody cares about upgrading unless they’re just ready to switch to USB-C.

62108705
>>62108666
Satanic digits

62108799
>>62108666
> Don’t Google why fraternity bros earn more money over the course of their lifetime than the average college graduate.
10% of Americans have 1m.
2.5% have 1m when you exclude a house.

62108879
>>62107202
I live in la jolla and i guess its considered a hcol city. Rent is $4k/month utilities are $200 electric is about $220

62108981
>>62107169
>newport 100
>mocha homosexual drink
You lost a game you didn’t even realize you were playing. Congrats

62110498
>>62108129
A smartphone and unlimited data is less than 1k a year, if you get a new phone every fucking year

Lead brained boomer.

62110818
>>62107317
Basically me although a lot of it is fueled by my wife and children. Some of it is that I’m a spoiled entitled little bitch too though. I have about 30k in credit card debt but I have 70k in crypto which used to be mid six figures so I’m hoping we get back somewhere close to that level before I sell to pay the credit cards off. Obviously it’s a huge gamble at this point and the lower we go the harder it is to continue holding.

62110913
>>62107317
>Americans expect to live like their parents/grandparents and are mad that the world’s greatest economy was stolen from them.
Seems pretty reasonable, actually

62110976
>>62107190
Yeah well the Jews had to go somewhere after WWII. I guess it's only fair they brought their tikkun olam to flood the cities with violent criminals and usurious debts to the U.S. The U.S. is almost fully healed i.e. an Africanized warzone. It's a painful process, but Europe is coming along great.

62111199
>>62108025
>You should be using all that money you would waste on rent, on investing
his $600 a month will be transformed into $660 next year by investing in the SPY.
surely that will help him leave poverty

people in this board are so fucking stupid lmao

62111384
>>62110818
That's me, but its 50k debt and currently 25k crypto. I was in a way better ratio position before nov '24 when I went ALL IN on what was supposed to be the obvious bull run. And have held all the way down
Alt szn save me before I have to an hero
>inb4 wtf were you thinking
ex wife took me to the cleaners and I put it all on the line to escape the state sponsored pussy jail serfdom

62111541
>>62110818
>$30k in credit card debt
>25%+ APR
>$70k in highly-volatile assets with no guaranteed return
>As opposed to a guranteed -25% return
>Doesn’t want to cash out
Behold the financial genius of /biz/.

62111611
>>62107169

62111774
>>62107937
he lives off 24k. he didnt say he made 24k. dipshit.
he should make about 54k assuming he works 40 hours a week

62111861
>>62107169
Proudest and comfiest goyslave award

62112010
>>62107169
Andy Nowicki is the best carfag on Youtube.

62112085
>>62110498
>>62108694
You don't need a smart phone.

62114440
>>62107317
I have never had a sick day or paid vacation day in my life.

62114446
>>62107169
gay post

62114448
this is a spam thread

62114452
bunch of fucking garbage spam in here which belongs on another board
probably alot of glowies talking to themselves

62114503
>>62107401
the tiktok watermark really makes this

62114662
>>62107202
this. OP is and always will be a faggot

62114990
>>62107169

item.title: any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management… — 77 replies — 20.6/hr
item.content:encoded:
any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management…

Open thread • Replies: 77 • Updated: 2026-04-14 11:55 UTC

OP

any WFH chads can you tell me how you found your job
I have hospitality, customer service and even low level management experience but im not getting any offers on my applications
Should I get some cyber security certs? which ones should I focus on if yes?

Early replies (first 3)

62084718
Funny of you to ask OP, I remember my struggles of landing my first WFH job. Btw, WFH stands for Work from Home because not many people come across that term usually. But, if you must ask, our company is headquartered in Nashville. My base pay is 95k per year along with a COL stipend and generous health and pension benefits. As we speak, I am clocked into my position in the beautiful hot springs in British Columbia. We do have an RTO policy, but I haven't been to the office in 4 years! Ha-Ha! You COULDN'T pay me to work in the office. Yesterday I had fun fucking a uni student that transferred from china for the school years, so yeah life is great so far. Today she is making me her traditional Chinese dinner as a thank you for fucking her so nicely. Maybe next month I'll fly out to Holland and do my job for the month. But like I said OP, keep your chin up and don't be disuaded by the multiple rejection emails in your future until you get that golden offer. Take it and run with it. Anyways .... Chiao!

62084729
>>62084718
thanks for the response anon
Did you use indeed or pay for a website that specializes in WFH jobs like flexjobs etc? I'm wondering if indeed is just all scams at this point and if it might be worth it to pay a job finding website to filter the garbage

62084746
There are no jobs anymore. 200,000 people have been laid off in the last six months while job creation is negative. 90% of job openings go to immigrants anyway.

Latest replies (last 30, oldest → newest)

62097875
>>62084718
This was me but moved to Poland on 125k USD / yr.

>>62084729
There are tons of remote job sites and even lang specific ones. I shifted to golang and can't imagine anyone works from the office in this lang.

62098209
>>62097875
>Poland on 125k USD / yr.
Damn, baller. Nobody in the U.S. would even interview me for remote cybersecurity jobs unless I was in certain states.

62100225
>>62097830
>it’s for females and minorities
That’s probably why your gay ass works in cyber security

62103272
>>62084678
Bump

62103921
>>62084678
I was on Fiver years ago, got banned after I agreed with a client to just work with them over email, and ive done it ever since.

62104035
>>62084678
I work in tech and not for public companies. Startup unicorns are still usually wfh

62104258
there aren't many positions where you can "work from home" and do shit all every day. your best bet is to apply to chainlink labs

62104775
>>62103921
How did they catch you breaking the rules?

62104981
>>62104775
Fiver scans all messages on their platform between you and clients to check if youre trying to pay without going through fiver

62105685
>>62104981
Can you just exchange socials and then setup the deal that way?

62105877
>>62105685
Bannable.

62106174
>>62104258
I thought you guys said chain link labs only hires pink hair HR ladies?

62106213
>>62084678
WFH "chads" are either larping or blind to how disposable they are seen

I highly suggest you stay out of jobs with that gimmick because HR salivates at the idea at eliminating any person who gets paid decently with some offshore alien. And to make matters worse theyll cut your hours and underhire intentionally so theres a mountain of shit to do while you fufill their harsh metrics.

The lack of commute is nice dont get me wrong(and I can especially imagine that if you live in a shithole like NYC or Cali) but it isnt worth just how unsecure your job is and also how utterly demanding it gets.

T. Worked 2 WFH jobs. The common theme between the two of them is that there was always a tremendous assload of work whenever I started shift and work itself was so aggressive I never wanted to do OT. I quit the second one in particular because during lull months for the company they cut my hours. When I started doing inperson work shit got a lot less stupid and I was ok working OT again.

62106222
>>62106213
Lack of commute is nice in shitholes like ATL, Dallas, or every other state in the country. Name one state that you would love to commute in

62106326
>>62106222
I think youre missing the point by asking that. It IS nice but not nice enough to put up with the rest of the garbage that comes with WFH. They are not jobs youre gonna go 20+ years with and retire on nor have that wonderous "dude I only do 4 hours of work a day in my pajamas and get paid for 12!" For long if at all. They are 100% either looking to get rid of your ass or practically turn you into a seasonal worker.

62106349
>>62106213
I *AM* the offshore alien. Work for an american Fortune 500 company. Full WFH and paid double the national average here in East Europe.

I remember being worried when Trump first became president that he will eliminate these jobs LOL

62106374
>>62097830

>apply for cybersec job
>mark black in the application
>don’t turn on your webcam during interview
>or use AI to make yourself black on screen

Why wouldn’t it work lol

62106382
>>62087410

This. Cybersec is a scam / DEI busywork that goes to (usually military) lunchtime rowdies in DC and Virginia, or foids. I got security+, cysa, pentest, network+, CEH etc, and a meme bachelors degree in cybersec back in 2022 and still haven’t gotten a job. Got about one offer since then, and it was a shit position for like $50k in the deep south. It’s also gatekept like hell. Even the reddit tier lefty white male hiring managers won’t hire you

Meanwhile you see foids and nogs on youtube, tiktok and linkedin bragging about their cushy $80k - $100k+ do-nothing cyber jobs and they have like one preliminary cert

62106486
>>62106174
yes, hence the recommendation. still not a woman, but wfh

62106871
>>62106326
Where are these non shithole cities anon

62106912
>>62106213
this only applies if you're in a low skill or white collar factory worker job (i.e., codetranny.) i work in a niche financial reporting role at a megacorp. fully remote and promoted several times to manager level roles despite the rest of my team being hybrid in another state. i'm not saying i'm indispensable or that no one could possibly do my job. i'm just saying that i have enough "high stakes" responsibility that i am immune to threats of RTO or offshoring.

62107877
>>62106382
>I can’t compete with minorities and women
Sounds like a you problem because I get offered jobs just because I’m white male and they hire mostly women so now I’m the DEI hire
Some of you guys just need better social skills and to lose some weight

62110424
>>62106912
Based and comfy

62112915
>>62084678
>>62084729
>>62084791
Upwork. I breezed through the coof pandemic thanks to freelance writing gigs.
If you want a cert, get an internationally recognized one.

62112917
>>62106486
You will never be a pink haired woman

62112921
>>62107877
What a cunt you are. You asshole nigger.

62112926
>>62112921
How much fried food have you eaten today?

62113047
>>62112926
A greater than zero value.

62113252
>>62105877
Lol

62114761
>>62105877
Fiver jannies suck
